old_claim.note: Genomic Epidemiology provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Environmental DNA extraction is documented in 1987, fifteen years before current pathogen genomic epidemiology emerged during SARS.

source_shape.source_locator: The review states that the first environmental-DNA reference dates to 1987 and concerned a method for extracting microbial DNA from sediments; the term expanded in the early 2000s.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The environmental-DNA review dates the first sediment microbial-DNA extraction method to 1987.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The older environmental sampling method cannot descend from the 2002 pathogen-surveillance field; both apply molecular data in different domains.

ontology_before: Environmental DNA monitoring depended on a later and domain-distinct epidemiology practice.
ontology_after: Environmental DNA depends on molecular analysis and ecology while remaining parallel to pathogen genomic epidemiology.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: environmental_dna_monitoring no longer directly depends on genomic_epidemiology.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to post-2002 pathogen eDNA surveillance derived specifically from genomic epidemiology.
Evidence moves the first environmental-DNA method after the SARS genomic-epidemiology milestone.
short_reason: Environmental DNA extraction is documented in 1987, fifteen years before current pathogen genomic epidemiology emerged during SARS.
